_meta.source_policy: PDF-derived learning content; story structure and characters are model_inference.
_meta.spoiler_warning: Do not open unless you want story spoilers.

spoiler_policy: Playable safe scenario text. Full final ending/result details are sealed.
title: Photon Relay: The Question Sayo Left Behind
logline: A local visual-novel science olympiad game where PDF-grounded prompts unlock a tournament story about assumptions, inheritance, and physical quantum information.

sessions.title: Qualifier: The Space Before the Answer

opening_scene.speaker: narration
opening_scene.text: The regional hall smelled like chalk dust, polished floor wax, and the kind of silence that made every page turn sound official.

opening_scene.speaker: haru
opening_scene.text: If I do not understand the notation, I am going to say so. Even if everyone hears me.

opening_scene.speaker: minori
opening_scene.text: Good. A wrong silence costs more than a wrong answer.

official_briefing.speaker: Senior Examiner
official_briefing.topic: state vectors, operators, and density operators
official_briefing.text: This foundation round tests the language of quantum mechanics: vector spaces, basis expansion, observables as operators, oscillator algebra, and density operators. Do not assume a symbol is a number just because it fits on one line. Ask what space it acts on, what basis is being used, and whether a subsystem has been separated from something unobserved.
official_briefing.what_not_to_assume: Do not treat non-commuting operators like ordinary algebraic variables.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: rikka
mid_scene_reaction.text: The answer is short. The missing reason is not.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: haru
mid_scene_reaction.text: Then I want the reason. If we cannot say it, we do not have it yet.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: minori
rival_pressure_scene.text: Sakuraba is naming its assumptions this year.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: haru
breakthrough_scene.text: A state is not just an answer waiting to be read. It is a promise about what measurements can mean.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: sayo
breakthrough_scene.text: That is the first page of the notebook I wanted to leave behind.

sessions.notebook_update: Haru writes: name the space, then the basis, then what the operator is allowed to do.
sessions.next_hook: Seiran's captain smiles at the oscillator board as if the next round has already begun.

sessions.title: Semifinal Pool: Light With Teeth

opening_scene.speaker: narration
opening_scene.text: The second hall had a darkened demonstration bench. One covered cavity sat beneath a glass case like a sleeping instrument.

opening_scene.speaker: touko
opening_scene.text: If your equation cannot tell me where the field lives, I am going to ask the apparatus instead.

official_briefing.speaker: Guest Researcher
official_briefing.text: You will move from oscillator language into electromagnetic modes, photon states, Rabi oscillations, and the Jaynes-Cummings model. The task is not to memorize names. You must distinguish classical-field approximations from quantized-field effects, and you must watch which excitation is being exchanged.
official_briefing.what_not_to_assume: Do not assume bright, coherent, number, and thermal fields have the same photon statistics.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: rikka
mid_scene_reaction.text: The mode is an oscillator. The atom is not the oscillator. That difference is the whole match.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: haru
mid_scene_reaction.text: So the field can be a partner, not just a push.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: touko
rival_pressure_scene.text: Where does your photon go if the mirror is not perfect? I will wait.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: rikka
rival_pressure_scene.text: Long enough for me to answer properly, I hope.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: haru
breakthrough_scene.text: The same symbol can feel different when the field stops being a background and starts keeping count.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: minori
breakthrough_scene.text: You noticed the change of model. That is worth points.

sessions.notebook_update: Haru marks two columns: classical drive and quantized mode. She leaves space between them.
sessions.next_hook: A cross-examination ticket arrives from Kuroha Academy: reservoir memory permitted.

sessions.title: Repechage: The Silent Reservoir

opening_scene.speaker: narration
opening_scene.text: Kuroha's table looked almost empty: two pens, one clock, and a stack of questions sharp enough to make the air feel thinner.

opening_scene.speaker: mio
opening_scene.text: Tell me what you traced out, and I will tell you whether I believe the answer.

official_briefing.speaker: Problem Committee Member
official_briefing.topic: open quantum systems, cavity loss, and propagation conditions
official_briefing.text: This round examines what happens when a small quantum system is not alone. You will need reduced density operators, reservoir assumptions, master-equation thinking, and the ability to compare coherent dynamics with loss. In the propagation problems, remember that the medium and field shape each other.
official_briefing.what_not_to_assume: Do not assume dissipation is a small afterthought; it can decide the observable result.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: sayo
mid_scene_reaction.text: Last year, we answered as if the environment had politely left the room.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: mio
rival_pressure_scene.text: Weak coupling, short memory, unchanged reservoir. Which of those are you actually using?

breakthrough_scene.speaker: haru
breakthrough_scene.text: The missing system still leaves fingerprints. If we ignore it, the clean equation becomes a story we told ourselves.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: mio
breakthrough_scene.text: That is the first answer today I did not want to interrupt.

sessions.notebook_update: Haru writes: the part you do not observe can still define the state you do observe.
sessions.next_hook: Akebono's borrowed textbook is open to the chapter on qubits.

sessions.title: Finalist Selection: Information That Cannot Be Copied

opening_scene.speaker: narration
opening_scene.text: By evening, the tournament hall had stopped feeling huge. It felt close, as if every team was leaning over the same fragile state.

opening_scene.speaker: yui
opening_scene.text: If we understand it alone, it does not count as understanding for our team.

official_briefing.speaker: Announcer
official_briefing.topic: qubits, gates, no-cloning, entanglement, and protocols
official_briefing.text: This session turns information into a physical object. You will be asked about qubit states, measurement, no-cloning, Bell states, teleportation, dense coding, and cryptographic basis choices. Keep the difference between a known classical label and an unknown quantum state clear.
official_briefing.what_not_to_assume: Do not assume entanglement sends usable messages faster than classical communication permits.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: haru
mid_scene_reaction.text: Not being able to copy it is not a weakness. It is the rule that makes the rest meaningful.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: rikka
mid_scene_reaction.text: You just said the theorem like it mattered to a person.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: yui
rival_pressure_scene.text: If your partner cannot follow the protocol, the protocol has not really arrived.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: haru
breakthrough_scene.text: Entanglement is not a shortcut around explanation. It is a stricter promise about the whole.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: minori
breakthrough_scene.text: You may be ready for the last room.

sessions.notebook_update: Haru writes: physical information has rules, and those rules can protect trust.
sessions.next_hook: The finalist board is covered before anyone can read the route.

sessions.title: Final Session: The Device That Has To Be Real

opening_scene.speaker: narration
opening_scene.text: The final session opened without music. Only a clean circuit diagram, a cavity sketch, and a list of allowed assumptions appeared on the screen.

opening_scene.speaker: sayo
opening_scene.text: This is the question I could not carry alone.

official_briefing.speaker: Chief Judge
official_briefing.topic: algorithms, error correction, and physical implementation
official_briefing.text: The final session asks you to connect abstract computation with physical systems. You may use algorithms, error correction, and implementation criteria, but every claim must identify what is being controlled, what decoheres, how readout works, and what assumptions make the model valid.
official_briefing.what_not_to_assume: Do not claim scalable computation from a single elegant effect.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: rikka
mid_scene_reaction.text: A circuit is not a machine until the physics can keep the promise.

mid_scene_reaction.speaker: haru
mid_scene_reaction.text: Then we answer as a team. Promise by promise.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: natsuki
rival_pressure_scene.text: If you omit one condition, the whole proof becomes decoration.

rival_pressure_scene.speaker: haru
rival_pressure_scene.text: Then I will not omit it.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: haru
breakthrough_scene.text: A quantum computer is not won by saying 'quantum' louder. It is built when the state, gates, errors, and measurements all survive the same argument.

breakthrough_scene.speaker: minori
breakthrough_scene.text: That answer belongs to this year's team.

sessions.notebook_update: Haru closes the notebook before the scoreboard. The answer is no longer only Sayo's unfinished question.
sessions.next_hook: The exact final result and ending route are sealed to preserve spoiler control outside play.
